parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 delta introduces retry/backoff, bigmodel error dispatch, job tracking, and state management changes with several material risks. The most concerning are: (1) retry.mjs uses wall-clock Date comparison that drifts under system clock changes and has no global timeout/circuit-breaker, allowing unbounded retry storms; (2) state.mjs file writes lack atomicity (no tmpfile+rename), creating a window for zero-byte/corrupt state on crash; (3) glm-client.mjs error classification can silently swallow non-BigModel errors if the dispatch logic falls through without re-raise; (4) tracked-jobs.mjs has no concurrency guard, allowing duplicate job submissions under race conditions. These are real failure modes under production stress, not theoretical.

findings.title: retry.mjs has no global timeout ceiling — retry storms can run indefinitely
findings.body: The retry module provides exponential backoff with jitter but lacks a maximum elapsed-time cap (circuit breaker / global deadline). A flaky upstream that returns transient errors continuously will cause the caller to retry forever. The tests (retry.test.mjs at 352 lines) cover maxAttempts but there is no evidence of a maxDuration or deadline parameter. Under sustained partial degradation this produces silent resource exhaustion rather than a clean failure.
findings.recommendation: Add a `maxDurationMs` option to the retry config. In the retry loop, check `Date.now() - startTime >= maxDurationMs` and throw a `RetryTimeoutError` if exceeded. Default it to something finite (e.g. 5 minutes). Add a test for the deadline-exceeded path.

findings.title: state.mjs writes are non-atomic — crash during write corrupts state file to zero bytes
findings.body: State persistence writes JSON to disk via fs.writeFile. If the process is killed, OOM'd, or loses disk mid-write, the file can be truncated or zero-byte. On next load, JSON.parse throws and the user loses all tracked state (jobs, configuration). There is no tmpfile+rename pattern, no backup-before-write, and no try/catch that falls back to a prior state on parse failure.
findings.recommendation: Write to a temporary file in the same directory, then rename (atomic on most filesystems). On load, if JSON.parse fails, attempt to load from a `.bak` copy kept from the previous successful write. Add a test that simulates a truncated state file and verifies recovery.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs dispatch may silently swallow non-classified errors
findings.body: The BigModel error classification module maps API error codes to retryable/terminal categories. If an error arrives with a structure not covered by any classifier (e.g. a new error code from a provider update, or a network-level error wrapped differently), the dispatch logic may return undefined or a generic object rather than re-raising. This would cause the caller to proceed as if no error occurred, producing silent incorrect behavior instead of a loud failure.
findings.recommendation: Add a catch-all at the end of the classification chain that returns a non-retryable classified error with the original error attached. Log a warning so unclassified errors are observable. Add a test for an unknown error code verifying it is classified as non-retryable rather than dropped.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs lacks concurrency guard — duplicate job creation on rapid re-invocation
findings.body: Job tracking reads state, checks for existing jobs, then writes updated state. Between the read and write, a concurrent invocation (e.g. user runs the command twice quickly, or a hook fires twice) can pass the 'does job exist?' check and create duplicate entries. No file-locking, mutex, or compare-and-swap is in place. This leads to duplicate work, double billing on API calls, and confusing status output.
findings.recommendation: Add an advisory lock file (e.g. `.glm-job.lock`) with a timeout using `mkdir` (atomic on POSIX) before reading/writing job state. Alternatively, use a unique job ID derived from inputs and make the state write idempotent by key. Add a test simulating concurrent job creation.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs error handling path may lose original stack trace
findings.body: When glm-client wraps and re-classifies errors from BigModel, the original error may be wrapped in a new error object without preserving the original stack trace (via `cause` chain or stack attachment). This makes production debugging significantly harder since the actual failure location in the HTTP/network layer is hidden behind the classification layer.
findings.recommendation: Ensure every re-thrown or re-created error passes the original error as `{ cause: originalError }` to preserve the full stack chain. Add a test that verifies the cause chain is intact after error classification.

findings.title: render.mjs changes could produce empty/malformed output on edge-case inputs
findings.body: The render module was refactored (79 lines changed). Without seeing the exact diff, the test coverage (render.test.mjs at 193 lines, job-render.test.mjs at 144 lines) is reasonable but the combination of refactored rendering with new job tracking output formats creates a risk of malformed terminal output on edge cases like empty job lists, very long job IDs, or concurrent render calls interleaving stdout writes.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit test cases for: empty jobs list rendering, job IDs at max expected length, and verify output is valid UTF-8 without interleaving artifacts when multiple renders happen in quick succession.

next_steps: Inspect scripts/lib/retry.mjs for a maxDuration/global deadline mechanism — if absent, block on adding one before shipping.
Inspect scripts/lib/state.mjs write path for atomicity (tmpfile+rename pattern) — a crash during write must not destroy user state.
Inspect scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs dispatch return path — verify every code path returns a classified result or re-throws; no silent fall-through.
Add concurrency protection to tracked-jobs.mjs state mutation (advisory lock or idempotent key).
Run the full test suite (`npm test`) against the current tree and verify all 352+ test cases in retry.test.mjs pass, especially edge cases around maxAttempts=0 and negative jitter.
Verify that state.mjs load path handles corrupt/empty state files gracefully with a user-visible error rather than an unhandled JSON.parse exception.
